---
title: "Hypothesis Testing: One-Way ANOVA"
author: "Karen Dworsky"
date: "2025-09-22"
format: html
execute:
  cache: true
---

## Setup

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(lterdatasampler)
library(rstatix)

data("and_vertebrates")
```

## Data Preparation

Filter for Coastal giant salamander across three channel unit types (C = cascade, P = pool, SC = side channel).

```{r}
salamander_data <- and_vertebrates %>%
  filter(species == "Coastal giant salamander",
         unittype %in% c("C", "P", "SC")) %>%
  select(unittype, length_1_mm) %>%
  filter(!is.na(length_1_mm), !is.na(unittype)) %>%
  mutate(unittype = as.factor(unittype))
```

## Assumption Checks

```{r}
salamander_data %>%
  group_by(unittype) %>%
  get_summary_stats(length_1_mm, type = "mean_sd")

normality_check <- salamander_data %>%
  group_by(unittype) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    shapiro_p = if (n >= 3 & n <= 5000) shapiro.test(length_1_mm)$p.value else NA,
    .groups = 'drop'
  )

normality_check

salamander_data %>%
  levene_test(length_1_mm ~ unittype)
```

## ANOVA and Post-Hoc Test

```{r}
anova_result <- salamander_data %>%
  anova_test(length_1_mm ~ unittype)

anova_result

if (anova_result$p < 0.05) {
  posthoc_result <- salamander_data %>%
    tukey_hsd(length_1_mm ~ unittype)
  posthoc_result
}

eta_squared <- salamander_data %>%
  anova_test(length_1_mm ~ unittype)

eta_squared$ges
```

## Visualization

```{r}
salamander_data %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = unittype, y = length_1_mm, fill = unittype)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", shape = 23, size = 3,
               fill = "white", color = "black") +
  labs(
    title = "Salamander Length by Channel Type",
    x = "Channel Type",
    y = "Snout-vent Length (mm)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```
